summary_text: Findings: - High: this looks like an expected scheduler guard, not a scheduler bug. `scheduler._validate_pre_post()` explicitly blocks posts under 800 chars for rows created after `2026-03-30` ([scheduler.py:335-338](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L335)). The failed row is `AIに、臨床の魂を`, `485` chars, `created_at=2026-04-03`, so the runtime did what it was designed to do. - High: the control failure is upstream. `can_approve()` does not check minimum length ([approval_guard.py:36-78](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py#L36)), and both `queue_audit` and the scheduler’s pre-due quarantine rely on `can_approve()` ([queue_audit.py:253-255](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L253), [scheduler.py:416-443](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L416)). That means a sub-800 post can be marked `approved` and only fail at due-time, which is exactly the recurrence risk you’re seeing. Safest remediation: - Treat the 800-char gate as an approval-time requirement, not just a due-time requirement. The safest fix is to make the approval path and audit path reject or quarantine sub-800 posts before they can sit in `approved`. - Add a regression test that an `approved` post with `created_at >= 2026-03-30` and `<800` chars is blocked before due-time, not just at post time. - If you need an operational stopgap before code change, run a queue audit over all `approved` rows and demote any sub-800 posts immediately. Sign-off recommendation: - No sign-off yet for recurrence prevention. The runtime guard is working as intended, but the approval pipeline is still letting invalid rows reach it.
